title: Running TypeScript
description: Learn how to compile and run TypeScript code.

slides.title: Browsers Don't Speak TS 🗣️
slides.content:
Here is the most important thing to know about TypeScript: **Browsers and Node.js cannot run TypeScript natively.**

They only understand JavaScript. So, how do we run our TypeScript code?

slides.title: Compilation (Transpilation) 🔄
slides.content:
Before our code can be executed, we must translate our TypeScript (`.ts`) files back into plain JavaScript (`.js`) files.

This process is called **compilation** or **transpilation**. We use the TypeScript Compiler, abbreviated as `tsc`, to do this.

quizData.question: Can Google Chrome run a `.ts` file directly?

options.text: No, it must be compiled into JavaScript first.
Only if Developer Tools are open.

quizData.explanation: Browsers only understand plain JavaScript, so TypeScript must be compiled before it can run in the browser.

slides.title: Using the Compiler
slides.content: Normally, you would install TypeScript on your computer and run compilation commands in the terminal. For example:

slides.title: The Output 📦
slides.content:
Running `tsc app.ts` reads your TypeScript file, strips out all the type information, makes sure there are no errors, and outputs a brand new file named `app.js`.

You then tell NodeJS to run `app.js` or link `app.js` in your HTML file!

*(Note: In this app, we handle the compilation behind the scenes for you!)*
